report.summary: Built 2 new learn guides — Tokenized Real Estate Guide 2026 (fractional property ownership, RealT/Lofty/Parcl/Propy comparison, $4T market projection) and Web3 Gaming & GameFi Guide 2026 (P2E economics, top games like Axie/Illuvium/Off The Grid, Ronin chain, $48B market) — with full SEO optimization, structured data, and internal linking; added both to PUBLISH_QUEUE.

sections.title: Research Brief
items: Tokenized real estate: Market projected to reach $4T by 2035; fractional ownership hitting $1.7T by 2026. RealT has 970+ properties ($150M+), Lofty targeting 12-15% APR DeFi yields, Parcl enabling real estate index trading. 80% of HNW investors already investing or planning to invest in tokenized assets.

Web3 gaming: Market valued at $33-48B in 2026 with 22.4% CAGR, projected $183B by 2034. P2E represents 42.26% of revenue. Ronin chain saw 80x DAU growth. Off The Grid won Game of the Year at GAM3 Awards 2025. Indie games expected to capture 70% of active Web3 players in 2026.

sections.title: Content Created
items: Tokenized Real Estate Guide 2026: 583-line comprehensive guide covering tokenization mechanics, 6 platform deep-dives (RealT, Lofty, Parcl, Propy, Landshare, Centrifuge), comparison tables, risk analysis, REITs vs tokenized comparison, and getting-started steps

Web3 Gaming & GameFi Guide 2026: 789-line guide covering P2E economics, 6 top game breakdowns (Axie, Pixels, Illuvium, Off The Grid, Shrapnel, STEPN), gaming blockchains (Ronin, Immutable X), indie revolution, and investment considerations

sections.title: SEO Optimization
items: Tokenized Real Estate: title 'Tokenized Real Estate Guide 2026 — Invest in Property with Crypto | degen0x', target keywords: tokenized real estate, fractional property ownership, RealT, Lofty, Parcl. Article + FAQ schemas (6 FAQs). ~3500 word count.

Web3 Gaming: title 'Web3 Gaming & GameFi Guide 2026 — Play-to-Earn, Top Games & Tokens | degen0x', target keywords: web3 gaming, GameFi, play to earn, P2E games 2026, Ronin chain. Article + FAQ schemas (6 FAQs). ~3800 word count.
Both pages include OpenGraph metadata, Twitter cards, breadcrumbs, structured data, and ToC with anchor links.
